judge_notes: All 8 cells pushed bench (1 commit ahead of base, local==remote tip) and pass `deno task check` (full gate: fmt, lint, type-check, tests, doc-lint, FR field-set, SALP, config drift). Functional CLI spot-checks run against fabricated /tmp fixtures. KEY DIFFERENTIATOR verified by judge: gpt-5.5-high and gpt-5.5-xhigh embed the full event history inside each state_snapshot, so compaction INCREASES file size (defeats the task's core goal of bounding journal growth); opus/fable/gpt-5.5-medium store only the run model and shrink the file.

cells.workflowFidelityNote: Exemplary task doc: A/B/C variant archetypes + reasoned selection, per-item DoD TDD trace with test+evidence, 8-point self-critique with triage, dual-reviewer (QA+lead) PASS verdict, FR registered in SRS section+index+map+design+README.

defects.desc: Auto-compaction verbose line printed via output.status, visible at normal verbosity too (verbose is a superset) — satisfies 'visible in verbose' but broader than literal.
defects.evidence: src/output.ts:45-50 status() returns only when verbosity==='quiet'

defects.desc: Item 7 proven via unparseable-prefix fixture rather than literal parse-count (equivalent/stronger proof).

cells.commitHygiene: Single focused feat(FR-E83) commit; src/mcp/commands.ts (+99) is in-scope shared compactRun core (alongside resumeRun/deliverHumanAnswer), not churn; no new deps.
cells.summary: Flawless. All 15 met with non-vacuous colocated tests, exact CLI behavior, exact 5-step protocol with per-step fault hook + crash-injection at all boundaries, single replayer extended, shrinking compaction, validated/documented config, in-scope MCP shared-core. Exemplary workflow record.

cells.workflowFidelityNote: Plan file with V1/V2/V3 variant analysis + rationale, per-DoD TDD trace to named tests, FR registration, verification commands.

defects.desc: tmp file fsync'd but not parent directory — spec's 5-step protocol does not require dir fsync, so spec-compliant.
Dry-run prints distinct 'dry-run: would compact ...' prefix rather than exact success line — spec reserves exact format for real success only; acceptable.

cells.workflowFidelityNote: Task doc: selected-variant rationale, 10-step RED/GREEN/REFACTOR TDD trace, DoD checklist with test+evidence, APPROVE review verdict, FR registered across SRS/SDS/index.

defects.desc: Snapshot embeds the full event history (`events: NonSnapshotRunJournalEvent[]`) so COMPACTION GROWS THE FILE (871 -> 1279 bytes in judge's live run) — the snapshot line carries every covered event verbatim, so journal byte-size still grows with run length. Defeats the task's stated core goal ('Long runs grow journal.jsonl without bound'). Replay line-count is still bounded (suffix-only holds), but the byte-reduction purpose is not met; bytesBefore->bytesAfter in the mandated summary line goes the wrong way.
defects.evidence: src/types.ts:636 `events: NonSnapshotRunJournalEvent[]`; run-journal.ts ~548 events_covered: replay.events.length with events stored verbatim; live: 871 -> 1279 bytes

cells.commitHygiene: Single conventional feat(engine) commit abed00c; clean tree; 22 cohesive files; no new deps.
cells.summary: Nominally all 15 met with exemplary workflow and exact CLI/protocol behavior, BUT the snapshot embeds the full event log, so compaction increases file size — a major design defect that undermines the feature's purpose. Ranked below the clean shrinking implementations despite 15/15.

cells.workflowFidelityNote: Thorough task doc: selected-variant rationale, 10-step RED/GREEN trace, per-DoD test+evidence, Review: Approve. Minor: only one variant explored ('non-interactive' rationale); index checkbox left unchecked.

defects.desc: Snapshot embeds the full event array (`events: RunJournalEvent[]`), so COMPACTION GROWS THE FILE (1500 -> 2027 bytes in judge's live run). Same core-goal-defeating defect as gpt-5.5-xhigh.
defects.evidence: src/types.ts:517 `events: RunJournalEvent[]`; run-journal.ts:434 `events: replay.events`; live: 1500 -> 2027 bytes

defects.desc: Auto-compaction runs a FULL journalCompactionDryRun (parse + full replay + serialize) on EVERY node completion before checking the threshold, so a long run re-replays the whole journal per node — O(n^2) total, the very cost the feature aims to bound.
defects.evidence: engine.ts maybeCompactJournal: stats computed via journalCompactionDryRun before `if (stats.events_since_snapshot <= threshold) return`

defects.desc: Commit message 'feat: add journal snapshots and compaction' omits the (engine) scope used by sibling commits; empty body.

cells.commitHygiene: Conventional Commits valid (type feat) but no scope and empty body; focused 15-file diff; plan file present.
cells.summary: Nominally 15/15 with solid tests, but carries TWO major defects: compaction grows the file (embedded event history) and the auto-trigger re-replays the entire journal on every node completion (O(n^2)) — both directly contradicting the task's bound-the-growth purpose. Weakest commit message among the set. Ranked last.
